titleStmt.title: The Consul General at Rangoon ( Packer ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Consul General at Rangoon ( Packer ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 486. June 5 handed Shwe Baw letter addressed to Aung San re consular district along line Deptel 204, June 2.1 referred conversation reported mytel 385, May 13,Not printed. and subsequent conversation with Aung San (my confdes 345, May 31See footnote 62, p. 25.) at which Shwe Baw was present during which Aung San said would be no difficulty in the matter. Shwe Baw to my surprise then (June 5) said Aung San understood I had been referring proposed consular convention. (I had covered matter consular district in informal memorandum re consular convention left with Aung San May 8—mytel 385.) Shwe Baw added, however, he foresaw no difficulties re consular district matter. I requested prompt action of [on] my letter.

Questioned by me on subsequent occasion Shwe Baw referred old question of China. I recalled to him Dept’s conversations British Embassy Washington (Deptel 286, November 26Not printed.) substance of which I sent to BickfordW. R. Bickford; then British Secretary of the Department of Defense and External Affairs. at the time in letter Shwe Baw had seen, emphatically said I felt Washington would take very unfavorable view any refusal our request and read him penultimate sentence Dept’s A–10, December 30.Not printed. He said he would see what could be done.

I believe favorable reply will be received shortly but nevertheless suggest Dept refrain from fixing time for public announcement (Deptel 211, June 6) re exchange diplomatic representatives until receipt information from me consular district matter settled satisfactorily.
Copies above-mentioned memorandum and GOB memorandum in reply (mytel 433, May 27See footnote 57, p. 23.) by pouch.Despatch 365, June 14, from Rangoon, not printed.
